repository_conventions.summary: Use getByRole, getByLabel, and getByTestId for element selection; avoid CSS class selectors

findings.title: CSS hash selector .btn-primary_a8f3d2 replaces semantic getByRole selector

evidence.summary: getByRole('button') replaced with locator('.btn-primary_a8f3d2') — the class name contains a hash suffix suggesting auto-generation

findings.why_it_matters: CSS class selectors with hash suffixes (like _a8f3d2) are typically auto-generated by CSS-in-JS tools and change with each build. This selector will break on the next style rebuild. The original getByRole('button', { name: 'Add to cart' }) selector is resilient to styling changes.
findings.suggested_fix: Restore the semantic selector: `await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Add to cart' }).click();`

findings.title: CSS hash selectors used for rating assertions

evidence.summary: Two locators use CSS class names with hash suffixes: .rating__stars_9k2m1 and .rating__count_x7p4q

findings.why_it_matters: Same issue as f-1. These selectors will break when styles are rebuilt. Add data-testid attributes to the rating elements or use getByRole/getByText alternatives.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `data-testid="rating-stars"` and `data-testid="rating-count"` to the rating elements in the component, then use `page.getByTestId('rating-stars')` and `page.getByTestId('rating-count')` in the test.

top_risks: tests/product.spec.ts introduces three CSS hash selectors that will break on the next style rebuild — restore semantic selectors or add data-testid attributes
